story.iWant: comprehensive documentation of how UniversalRecipe parameters map to DNG Camera Profile (DCP) tone curves and color matrices
story.soThat: I understand the conversion formulas, supported/unsupported features, and can verify correct DCP generation from any source format (NP3, XMP, lrtemplate, .costyle)
story.tasks: - Task 1: Document DCP Parameter Mappings (AC-1) - 5 subtasks - Task 2: List Unsupported DCP Features (AC-2) - 4 subtasks - Task 3: Define Tone Curve Formulas (AC-3) - 3 subtasks - Task 4: Document Color Matrix Handling (AC-4) - 4 subtasks - Task 5: Create Cross-Format Examples (AC-5) - 3 subtasks - Task 6: Test with Real Adobe DCP Samples (AC-6) - 7 subtasks - Task 7: Create Reference Documentation (AC-7) - 4 subtasks - Task 8: Manual Validation - 3 subtasks

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: - Add "DCP (DNG Camera Profile)" section to docs/parameter-mapping.md - Document all supported UniversalRecipe parameters that map to DCP (Exposure, Contrast, Highlights, Shadows) - Specify exact conversion formulas with code examples - Include visual diagrams showing linear → adjusted curve transformations - Document precision considerations (float → int curve points, rounding)

- List DCP features NOT implemented in Recipe v0.1.0 (dual illuminant, camera calibration, HSV tables, etc.) - Explain why each feature is unsupported (identity matrices used instead) - Note Recipe uses identity color matrices (no color calibration) - Document fallback behavior (unsupported parameters ignored)

- Document 5-point tone curve generation algorithm with step-by-step formula - Include worked examples (exposure +0.5, contrast +0.3, etc.) - Document monotonic curve enforcement (output[i] >= output[i-1]) - Explain clamping to 0-255 range

- Explain identity matrix usage (3x3 diagonal matrix) - Clarify this means no color space transformation (camera RGB → XYZ passthrough) - Explain ProfileCalibrationSignature = "com.adobe" (standard signature) - Note Recipe focuses on tone/exposure only (not color calibration) - Document when full calibration would be needed (out of scope for v0.1.0)

- Include minimum 3 example conversions (NP3→DCP, XMP→DCP, lrtemplate→DCP) - Show complete UniversalRecipe intermediate step - Include visual before/after curve diagrams - Document expected precision (±1 curve point due to rounding)

criterion.title: Test Mappings with Real Adobe DCP Samples

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: - Load minimum 3 real DCP files from Adobe (Camera Raw, Lightroom) - Parse tone curves from real samples - Reverse-engineer curve → UniversalRecipe parameters - Generate new DCP from derived UniversalRecipe - Compare original vs. generated curves (visual + numerical) - Document findings in parameter-mapping.md - Store real DCP samples in testdata/dcp/adobe-samples/ with README

- Add comprehensive "DCP Parameter Mapping" section to parameter-mapping.md - Include references to Adobe DNG Specification 1.6 - Add glossary terms (IFD, Tone Curve, Color Matrix, Illuminant, HSV table) - Link to implementation files (dcp/generate.go, dcp/parse.go) - Update docs/index.md with DCP section link

doc.title: Epic 9 Technical Specification
doc.section: Epic 9 adds DCP (DNG Camera Profile) format support to Recipe
Tone curve conversion formulas, 5-point curve structure
Parse and Generate functions for DCP format
AC-3 specifically covers DCP Parameter Mapping documentation

doc.title: Parameter Mapping Rules
doc.snippet: Document structure to follow for DCP section: Overview, Supported Parameters, Conversion Formulas, Examples

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.section: Understanding tone curves, HSL adjustments, color transformations

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.section: internal/formats/{format}/ pattern with parse.go and generate.go
Table-driven tests with real sample files

doc.title: Story 9-1: DCP Parser
doc.section: AC-3 describes tone curve extraction formulas
doc.snippet: Parser analyzes curve shape to extract exposure, contrast, highlights, shadows

doc.title: Story 9-2: DCP Generator
doc.section: AC-3 describes tone curve generation algorithm with exact formulas
doc.snippet: 5-point curve generation: linear base → apply exposure → apply contrast → apply highlights/shadows

artifact.reason: Contains tone curve generation implementation to reference in documentation
Example of tone curve parsing pattern from existing format
Shows tone curve adjustment formulas (contrast, highlights, shadows, whites, blacks)
Example of complete format generation documentation pattern
Shows tone curve point generation for similar format

go.package: XML marshaling/unmarshaling for Adobe Camera Profile XML
Math operations for tone curve calculations

constraints.constraint: DCP Parameter Mapping section must follow established pattern from other formats (XMP, NP3, costyle) in parameter-mapping.md
Documentation formulas must exactly match implementation in dcp/generate.go (Story 9-2)
Document ±1 curve point precision tolerance due to float→int rounding
MVP focuses on tone curves only - no full camera calibration (ForwardMatrix, ColorMatrix)
Minimum 3 real Adobe DCP samples required for validation (Camera Raw, Lightroom, or DNG SDK)

interface.name: parameter-mapping.md
interface.signature: Add "DCP (DNG Camera Profile)" section following established structure

interface.name: testdata/dcp/adobe-samples/
interface.signature: Directory containing real Adobe DCP samples with README explaining sources

interface.name: docs/index.md
interface.signature: Add link to DCP parameter mapping documentation

tests.standards: Documentation story - no unit tests required. Validation through: - Technical accuracy review (formulas match dcp/generate.go implementation) - Real sample analysis (parse 3 Adobe DCPs, reverse-engineer parameters, regenerate) - Cross-reference verification (all internal links work) - Usability testing (can developer understand DCP conversion from docs alone?)
tests.locations: - docs/parameter-mapping.md (updated with DCP section) - testdata/dcp/adobe-samples/ (real DCP samples for analysis)

ideas.idea: Manual validation: Verify formulas in documentation match dcp/generate.go implementation using code inspection and worked examples in spreadsheet
Create worked example: Take exposure +0.5, contrast +0.3, highlights -0.2, shadows +0.1 and calculate step-by-step through the algorithm to verify accuracy
Test cross-format examples: Convert NP3 sample → UniversalRecipe → DCP, then verify tone curve values match documented formulas
Real sample analysis: Parse adobe-standard.dcp, reverse-engineer parameters, regenerate DCP, compare curves point-by-point, document max delta
Documentation completeness: Check all glossary terms are used in documentation, all cross-references resolve correctly, all code examples are syntactically correct
